summary: High‑energy Fall ’89 Spectrum show with a monster Other One jam and solid soundboard capture despite occasional mix quirks.
review: The performance captures the band at a creative peak – the 15‑minute Other One excursion, a searing California Earthquake, and a tight Hey Pocky Way > Scarlet > Fire sequence make the second set unforgettable.  The DSBD source is generally clear and balanced, though a few audience splices and an overly bright symbols mix give parts of the show a slightly rough edge.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (30+) rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise the music; recording is described as good with only isolated mix issues, yielding a weighted rating of ~4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced DSBD capture; occasional audience splices, low volume in the early second set, and a harsh high‑frequency “symbols” mix on drums noted by several reviewers.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, inspired first set sets up a spectacular second set; the Other One jam anchors the middle, while an ill‑timed I Will Take You Home break slightly disrupts momentum before the finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Occasional vocal slip-ups and lyric forgetfulness on Fire on the Mountain; otherwise strong lead guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeated “intro bomb” on The Other One, helping launch the epic jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard and MIDI work receive high praise – bright, inventive, though at times mixed too loud.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum mix (“symbols”) is noted as overly bright/high in the mix; otherwise solid rhythmic foundation.
